LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS JOHN HARTFORD AND ALBERT LEE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS ON FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.(SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR FULL TEXT.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH KARNAZES' VERIFIED PETITION FOR ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE A VERIFIED AMENDED COMPLAINT AGAINST ATTORNEYS CONTAINING ALLEGATIONS OF DAMAGES ARISING FROM CIVIL CONSPIRACY IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S PETITION TO FILE A VERIFIED AMENDED COMPLAINT, WITHOUT PREJUDICE, BECAUSE THERE IS NOT A REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PREVAIL ON THE FIRST THROUGH TENTH CAUSES OF ACTION. (SEE HUNG V. WANG (1992) 8 CAL.APP.4TH 908.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR FULL TEXT.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Continued To Mar-15-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On First Amended Complaint And First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Elizabeth Karnazes telephone number 650-345-9200 appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Attorney Albert Lee telephone number 415-215-6868 specially appeared for Defendants.

Ruling: The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JOHN HARTFORD and ALBERT LEE'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons On First Amended Complaint And First Amended Complaint is granted. In order for the summons on the first amended complaint to be valid, the summons needed to be signed by the clerk and issued under the seal of the court. (Code of Civil Procedure sections 412.20(a) and 153.2.) The court grants Defendants' motion to quash service of summons on the first amended complaint because Plaintiff failed to comply with Code of Civil Procedure sections 412.20(a) and 153.2. The motion is also granted on the grounds that Plaintiff failed to obtain a filing order pursuant to Civil Code section 1714.10. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

Verified Petition For Order Granting Leave To File A Verified Amended Complaint Against Attorneys Containing Allegations Of Damages Arising From Civil Conspiracy
Off Calendar Nov-19-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Karnazes telephone number 650-345-9200 appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Attorney Albert Lee telephone number 415-215-6868 specially appeared for Defendants.

Ruling: The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH KARNAZES' Verified Petition For Order Granting Leave To File A Verified Amended Complaint Against Attorneys Containing Allegations Of Damages Arising From Civil Conspiracy is denied without prejudice. The court denies Plaintiff's petition to file a verified amended complaint, without prejudice, because there is not a reasonable probability that Plaintiff will prevail on the first through tenth causes of action. (See Hung v. Wang (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 908.) The first through tenth causes of action are barred by collateral estoppel because they are all premised on the allegation that Defendants and Plaintiff reached a settlement agreement and that Defendants subsequently breached the agreement. (First Amended Complaint page 4-23.) The issues of whether Plaintiff and Defendants formed a settlement agreement and whether Defendants breached the agreement were already litigated in the San Mateo Superior Court and were determined against Plaintiff. (Lodgment of Exhibits in Opposition to Petition for Leave to File a Verified Amended Complaint Against Attorneys Containing Allegations of Damages Arising from Conspiracy: Exhibits 14, 17, 18.) Although the eleventh through fourteenth causes of action are based partly on the alleged settlement agreement, those causes of action also raise claims that Defendants filed improper liens against Plaintiff's home. The San Mateo action did not resolve the issue of whether Defendants filed improper liens against Plaintiff's home. Thus, the court denies Plaintiff's petition for order granting leave to file a verified amended complaint, without prejudice. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)
